title: Determining the sign of a trig ratio from the quadrant
problem: Without using a calculator, determine the sign of $\sin 250°$ and state which quadrant the angle lies in.

steps.instruction: Identify the quadrant by comparing $250°$ with the quadrant boundaries.
steps.working: The quadrant boundaries are $0°$, $90°$, $180°$, $270°$, $360°$. Since $180° < 250° < 270°$, the angle lies in the third quadrant.

steps.instruction: Recall which ratios are positive in the third quadrant using the ASTC rule.
steps.working: ASTC: All positive (Q1), Sin positive (Q2), Tan positive (Q3), Cos positive (Q4). In the third quadrant, only $\tan$ is positive; $\sin$ and $\cos$ are both negative.
Since $250°$ is in the third quadrant and $\sin$ is negative there, $\sin 250° < 0$.

title: Using the supplementary angle identity for an obtuse angle
problem: Find the exact value of $\cos 135°$ using the supplementary angle identity.

steps.instruction: Express $135°$ in the form $180° - \theta$ to identify the related acute angle.
steps.working: $135° = 180° - 45°$, so the related acute angle is $\theta = 45°$.

title: Simplifying an expression using reflection identities
problem: Use the identities $\sin(360° - \theta) = -\sin\theta$ and $\cos(-\theta) = \cos\theta$ to simplify and evaluate $\sin(360° - 30°) \times \cos(-60°)$.

steps.instruction: Apply the identity $\sin(360° - \theta) = -\sin\theta$ with $\theta = 30°$ to simplify the first factor.
Apply the identity $\cos(-\theta) = \cos\theta$ with $\theta = 60°$ to simplify the second factor.
Multiply the two simplified values to find the exact result.
